title: Dense British Sponge Cake Trifle
description: A rich and rewarding brunch dish featuring multiple layers of sponge cake, fruit, and custard, finished with a sprinkle of nutmeg.
instructions: Preheat the oven to 375°F (190°C) and grease a 9-inch (23cm) round cake pan.
Beat the butter and sugar together until light and fluffy, then beat in the eggs one at a time.
In a separate bowl, whisk together the flour and milk, then add the vanilla extract and mix well.
Gradually add the flour mixture to the butter mixture and mix until just combined.
Pour the batter into the prepared cake pan and smooth the top.
Bake for 25-30 minutes, or until a toothpick inserted into the center of the cake comes out clean.
Allow the cake to cool in the pan for 5 minutes, then transfer it to a wire rack to cool completely.
To make the custard, combine the heavy cream and granulated sugar in a saucepan and heat over medium heat, stirring constantly, until the sugar has dissolved and the mixture is hot but not boiling.
Remove the custard from the heat and let it cool to room temperature, then refrigerate until chilled.
To assemble the trifle, cut the cooled cake into 1-inch (2.5cm) cubes and layer them in a large bowl with the sliced strawberries and blueberries.
Pour the chilled custard over the fruit and cake, then sprinkle with grated nutmeg.
Refrigerate the trifle for at least 30 minutes before serving.
